violations.description: General employee practices. — --Food employee observed without proper hair restraint. -Cook with beard was not wearing a beard net.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not properly protected from potential contamination. -Chili cooling uncovered next to walk in in cooler fans. -Ice trays in walk in freezer are not covered.--Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. -Tray of garnish items stored on floor below hand sink at bar.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Debris/residue at bar walk in cooler fans. -Bottom interior of salad prep table has food/debris build up. -Dust/debris at main walk in cooler fans. -Build up of grease/debris/carbon at oven interior.
Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Equipment/utensil was not air-dried or allowed adequate draining after sanitizing. -Observed dishwasher in kitchen towel dry dishes.
Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Warewash machine was not maintained and/or operated as required. -Bar dishwasher found at 25-50ppm Chlorine, appears to need recalibration.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. -Sleeve of napkins on floor at bar storage closet. -Kitchen back room has single use straws and other items stored below exposed plumbing lines. -Case of kids cups stored on floor in back room.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. -Items at bottom of salad prep cooler found with temperature of 45-46F. -Small make table at cookline had temperature of 45F at front top minced garlic. *Keep top closed when not in use. -Sauces in ice bath found with bottom temperatures of ~36F to top temperatures of 50F. *Stir frequently and refresh ice as needed. -Whipped margarine "left out to soften for portioning" found with temperature of 64F to 67F. -Server refrigerator set to 50F with milk found at 50F. *Unit reset to 39F while on-site.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. Raw beef patties being stored above ready to eat food in the walk-in cooler.

Person in charge. — --Certified food service manager had an expired certificate or is no longer associated with this establishment. Currently there is no one with a serv safe certificate, the owner is scheduled to take the test March 3rd.

General employee practices. — --Employee failed to maintain a high degree of personal cleanliness or observe proper hygienic practices. Observed the employee washing dishes handle dirty dishes and then handle clean dishes without washing hands, also observed him wash his hands while wearing gloves. Employee cutting lettuce did not wash his hands after changing out his gloves.
Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Boxes of food being stored on the walk-in freezer floor.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. Salad prep table top was temping at 43.1 degrees and the underneath was temping at 46.5.

Hot storage of potentially hazardous food. — --Hot food storage facilities/equipment was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining temperature of potentially hazardous food at 140F or greater. Hotbox holding soup was only holding at 125 degrees.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Several containers of food and open packages of food were not labeled or marked.

Variance requirement for specialized processing methods. — --Food establishment did not obtain approved variance or HACCP plan from regulatory authority for special process of reduced oxygen packaging. Facility is vacuum sealing raw beef, raw fish, and raw pork. Facility is currently doing a pork sous-vide.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. Food items being stored on cardboard in the walk-in freezer. Shelves in the server station are warp and pitted.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Food contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in good repair or sound condition. Gasket on the stand up freezer and small prep cooler are broken. Cutting board are stain and pitted. Glass bowl being used to scoop rice out of the rice maker, not designed with a handle to be used as a scoop.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Inside of the stand up freezer, racks in the walk-in cooler, inside of the oven on the cook line, on top of the heat lamp next to the cook line, and walk-in cooler fans all need a thorough cleaning.

Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Equipment/utensil was not air-dried or allowed adequate draining after sanitizing. Observed dishwasher drying dishes with towels and placing them on a rack. Cutting boards are not being alowed to dry before being placed back on the prep tables.
Prep/vegetable sink. — --Prep sink was not plumbed with a physical air gap on the drain line. (located at 3 compartment sink)

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Floors in all the walk-in coolers, floors in the walk-in freezer and floors on the cook line need to be cleaned. High dust throughout kitchen. Wall behind cooking equipment needs cleaned.--Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not maintained in good repair. Floors in the kitchen are worn through with pitting underneath the wheels of kitchen equipment. Light wrapped in duct tape under the hood system needs to be fixed.
Control of insects, rodents, and other pests. — --Insect electrocution or trapping device was installed in an inappropriate location. Fly strip located above the 3 compartment sink.

Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
